 {"id":"frankenredis-ajk","title":"Implement real XINFO CONSUMERS pending count and idle/inactive time","description":"## Problem

fr-store/src/lib.rs line 5796: xinfo_consumers returns Vec<Vec<u8>> (just consumer names).
fr-command/src/lib.rs lines 4169-4179: stream_consumer_info_to_frame hardcodes pending=0, idle=0, inactive=0.

## What Redis Returns

XINFO CONSUMERS key group returns per-consumer:
- name: consumer name
- pending: number of PEL entries owned by this consumer
- idle: milliseconds since last interaction (XREADGROUP/XCLAIM/XACK)
- inactive: milliseconds since last successful XREADGROUP (Redis 7.2+)

## Implementation Plan

1. Change Store::xinfo_consumers to return Vec<(Vec<u8>, usize, u64, u64)> — (name, pending_count, last_interaction_ms, last_read_ms)
2. Compute pending_count by scanning the PEL and counting entries where consumer matches
3. Track last_interaction_ms per consumer in StreamGroupState (update on XREADGROUP, XCLAIM, XACK)
4. Track last_read_ms per consumer (update only on XREADGROUP with >)
5. Update stream_consumer_info_to_frame to use real values

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: StreamGroupState consumer tracking, xinfo_consumers
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs: stream_consumer_info_to_frame","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:21.561101730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:44.406972571Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:44.406145205Z","close_reason":"XINFO CONSUMERS now returns real pending count and idle time computed from PEL, not hardcoded zeros","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-ajk","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-7o5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:31.307893656Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-as1","title":"Wire CONFIG SET maxclients to fr-server connection acceptance","description":"## Problem

fr-server/src/main.rs line 33 defines `const MAX_CLIENTS: usize = 10_000` and uses it at line 499 to reject new connections. CONFIG SET maxclients updates the config table in fr-runtime but never propagates to fr-server's actual enforcement.

## What Redis Does

Redis's CONFIG SET maxclients immediately changes the rlimit and connection acceptance threshold. New connections beyond the limit get an error response and are closed.

## Implementation Plan

1. Add a `max_clients` field to `ServerState` in fr-runtime (initialized to 10000)
2. In `handle_config_set`, when maxclients is set, update `self.server.max_clients`
3. In fr-server's main loop, read `runtime.server.max_clients` (or expose via a getter) instead of the const
4. Also update `store.server_maxclients` so INFO reports the correct value
5. Remove the `MAX_CLIENTS` const from fr-server

## Files to Modify

- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`: Add field to ServerState, handle in config_set
- `crates/fr-server/src/main.rs`: Replace const with runtime read
- `crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs`: Already has server_maxclients field (added in f8c5a8a)

## Testing

- Unit test: CONFIG SET maxclients 100 → CONFIG GET maxclients returns 100
- Unit test: INFO clients section reports the new maxclients value
- Integration: verify connection rejection beyond the configured limit (may need fr-conformance fixture)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:25.451789990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:12.846797806Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:12.845683417Z","close_reason":"maxclients wired: ServerState.max_clients + CONFIG SET/GET + fr-server reads runtime value","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-as1","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-kgn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:28.584373097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"frankenredis-s5su","title":"HIGH: RDB type 15 (stream) encoding diverges from upstream — file interchange broken","description":"## Summary

Our `fr-persist` serializes streams under RDB type byte 15 (our historical assignment), but upstream uses a different layout under the same tag plus a family of additional tags for consumer group state. Project memory note `project_rdb_stream_incompatibility.md`: \"RDB type 15 stream encoding differs from Redis, breaking file interchange\".

Impact: any user who takes an upstream `dump.rdb` and boots our server on it gets a parse failure or (worse) silently-truncated stream state. Symmetrically, our RDB cannot be loaded by upstream. That violates AGENTS.md Compatibility Doctrine strict mode, which promises \"maximize observable compatibility for V1-scoped APIs\" with \"no behavior-altering repairs\".

## Surface to verify

Upstream `rdb.h` / `rdb.c` (see `legacy_redis_code/redis/src/rdb.h` and `rdb.c`):

- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS` (15) — listpack-bodied stream
- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_2` (19) — adds per-consumer seen_time
- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3` (21) — adds per-consumer active_time (7.2+)

Each shape writes:
1. radix tree: (stream-id, listpack)
2. stream metadata: length, last_id, first_id, max_deleted_id, entries_added
3. per consumer group: name, last_id, PEL size, PEL entries, consumer list
4. per consumer: name, seen_time (v2+), active_time (v3+), pending entry IDs

Our encoder should write v3 by default (matches upstream 7.2+) and decode all three.

## Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses + /testing-fuzzing

**Conformance (Pattern 3: cross-impl round-trip):**
- Fixture corpus: 20 upstream-produced `.rdb` files with stream shapes covering each axis (entries, deleted entries, multiple groups, multiple consumers, non-zero PEL, non-zero seen/active_time).
- Round-trip gate:
  1. `fr-persist::rdb::decode(bytes)` must succeed on every fixture.
  2. Re-encode; feed to upstream `redis-server --loadrdb`, query `XINFO STREAM FULL`; canonicalize and compare to our canonicalized `XINFO STREAM FULL` post-load.
  3. Reverse: encode our in-memory stream, have upstream load it, compare stream state vector.

**Fuzzing (/testing-fuzzing, structure-aware):**
- Extend the existing `fuzz_rdb_structured.rs` with a stream-producing `arbitrary::Arbitrary` generator: random entries, groups, PELs. Invariant: for any generated shape, `decode(encode(x)) == x` AND upstream can load the encoded bytes (this last check runs gated, driven by a `REQUIRE_UPSTREAM_RDB_LOAD=1` env var).
- Extend `fuzz_rdb_decoder.rs` with upstream-produced corpus (minimized from the 20 fixtures above) so crashes on adversarial streams are found quickly.

## Related

- Frames our broader persistence story for streams; the `core_stream.json` conformance fixtures already exist (`crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_stream.json`, 114 KB).
- Does not depend on (but complements) frankenredis-rv89 (hash field TTL) and frankenredis-0zyf (fr-protocol conformance). All three share the upstream-fixture harvesting infra.

## Acceptance

- 20/20 upstream fixtures decode cleanly.
- Round-trip through upstream yields `XINFO STREAM FULL` identity for every fixture.
- Fuzz target logs zero crashes / zero mismatches over 1M iterations.

## Labels

 {"id":"frankenredis-ug8k","title":"EPIC: Implement real cross-process replication (PSYNC full-resync + partial-resync + live streaming)","description":"# Replication Epic

## Why This Is Critical
FEATURE_PARITY.md claims "parity_green" for replication, but this is false. The state machine logic (665 lines in fr-repl) is tested in isolation, but:
- REPLICAOF returns "OK" without actually connecting to a primary
- No bytes ever flow between two FrankenRedis processes
- Cannot replicate from a real Redis primary
- A real Redis replica cannot connect to FrankenRedis as primary
- Zero cross-process replication tests exist

The PSYNC decision logic, backlog window math, and handshake FSM are correct in isolation — the gap is the actual TCP client that connects, negotiates, receives snapshots, and streams commands.

## What Success Looks Like
1. `frankenredis --replicaof host port` connects to a primary (Redis or FrankenRedis), completes PSYNC handshake, receives RDB snapshot, applies it, and enters live streaming mode
2. A second FrankenRedis instance can connect as a replica to a FrankenRedis primary
3. Writes to the primary appear on the replica within bounded latency
4. Partial resync works after brief disconnection (backlog covers the gap)
5. An integration test demonstrates this end-to-end

## Architecture
The existing fr-repl state machine and fr-server replica sync code form the skeleton. The missing pieces are:
1. Real TCP client connection in REPLICAOF handler (currently a no-op)
2. Backlog buffer that actually stores propagated commands
3. Command propagation: after each write command, serialize and push to replica write buffers
4. Snapshot streaming: encode current store as RDB and send to new replicas
5. Live command streaming: send write commands to connected replicas in real-time

## Non-Goals for V1
- Multi-master / cluster replication
- Sentinel integration
- Diskless replication (can always go through RDB file)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.794017436Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:18:38.527795780Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:18:38.527541499Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks complete.  {"id":"frankenredis-xvvu","title":"Wire real counters into INFO stats section","description":"# Wire real counters into INFO stats section

## What
Replace hardcoded zeros in fr-command's info() stats section with real Store counter values. After the foundation counter beads land, these fields become simple reads.

## Fields to fix (fr-command/src/lib.rs, info() function, stats section ~line 8290):
1. expired_keys → store.stat_expired_keys
2. expired_stale_perc → store.stat_expired_stale_perc (or compute from cycle stats)
3. expired_time_cap_reached_count → 0 is acceptable (cap not implemented)
4. expire_cycle_cpu_milliseconds → store.stat_expire_cycle_cpu_milliseconds
5. evicted_keys → store.stat_evicted_keys
6. total_keys_expired → store.stat_expired_keys (same counter)
7. total_keys_evicted → store.stat_evicted_keys (same counter)
8. keyspace_hits → store.stat_keyspace_hits
9. keyspace_misses → store.stat_keyspace_misses

## Fields in clients section (~line 8198):
10. blocked_clients → store.stat_blocked_clients (populated by server)
11. tracking_clients → 0 (acceptable until CLIENT TRACKING is fully wired)

## Fields in memory section (~line 8218):
12. maxmemory → store.maxmemory_bytes_live (populated by runtime)

## Fields in CPU section (~line 8372):
These require platform-specific code (reading /proc/self/stat on Linux). Lower priority — acceptable to leave as 0 with a comment explaining why.

## Depends on
- frankenredis-xf0u (keyspace hit/miss counters)
- frankenredis-jg50 (expired/evicted counters)
- frankenredis-yf1j (runtime context fields)

## Testing
- Conformance: add INFO stats cases that verify non-zero counters after operations
- Unit test: set + get key, verify keyspace_hits incremented in INFO output

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (info() function stats/clients/memory sections)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:16.554565415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T03:07:43.430353494Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T03:07:43.430099193Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-xvvu","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-jg50","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:34.361555156Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-xvvu","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-xf0u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:34.243078331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-xvvu","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-yf1j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:34.479597319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
